concerns.text: FR-001 mandates downloading numeric datasets from the UCI Machine Learning Repository. The tasks (T016-T024) only implement synthetic parametric distribution generators (LogNormal, Beta, t-distribution). There is no task to implement UCI dataset downloading, parsing, or selection logic.

FR-002 requires selecting continuous variables with no missing values from the UCI dataset. Tasks T016-T024 focus on synthetic data generation and do not include logic for filtering real-world dataset columns or handling missing values in external data.

The spec defines edge cases for UCI datasets: skipping datasets with <30 rows, excluding rows with missing values, and filtering categorical variables. The tasks (Phase 3) implement synthetic generators which inherently avoid these issues, leaving no task to handle the specified UCI edge cases.

FR-010 requires treating the UCI dataset as a realization from a super-population and sampling with replacement. The tasks implement sampling from synthetic distributions (T016) rather than sampling with replacement from a loaded UCI dataset array.

User Story 1 explicitly states the system must draw samples from 'full UCI datasets'. The implementation tasks (T016-T024) only cover drawing from synthetic parametric distributions, creating a gap where the core data source requirement is unimplemented.

Tasks T016-T024 are tagged with [US1] but describe implementation for synthetic data (LogNormal, Beta, t-distribution) which contradicts the [US1] definition in spec.md that requires UCI datasets. This indicates a stale or mismatched task definition relative to the current spec.
